pages.title: About
pages.text: Who am IWillkommen! I am frostwing98, enrolled in Nanjing University in 2016, majoring in Software Engineering. I am interested in Network Attack and Defence, as well as the CTF contests. Currently, I am engaged in undergraduate research training under supervision of Prof. Fengyuan Xu and Asst. Prof. Hongyu Kuang. See my CV for more details. Research, Projects and ServicesResearchCurrently I am collaborating with two brilliant teachers and advancing in two interesting fields: Prof. Fengyuan Xu in COSEC, and Assistant Prof. Hongyu Kuang, in Software Institute. I am interested in two different fields: Cybersecurity, especially in verification of learning models and privacy preserving, and, Information Retrieval, especially from Information generated during Software Development: I have started a new project with Assoc Prof. Jingyu Hua. It is about how to use side channel information to localize certain hidden digital device that may be secretly filming in public hotels. In the research of learning model security, I am co-operating with Prof Xu, and Hao Wu, who is currently pursuing his phd. degree in CS department. In the software information retrieval research, I am cooperating with Assistant Prof. Kuang, leading a tiny group with two students who is expected to graduate this summer for their graduation thesis. I also completed a project with supervision of Assoc. Prof. Jingyu Hua: the MAIL indoor AP localization project. The project achieved great accuracy. Moreover, I am also leading a 9-person group under a comprehensive empirical software engineering literature review project. Besides, I am a member of the Nanjing University Trinity CTF team previously. Please note: further discussions and details of my research interest ,status and plans will be included in my research proposal, which is expected to be available before July. ProjectsI had done or am doing several projects according to my own personal interests. HiddenMosaic: Adversarial Sample Generation for Deep Learning Models MAIL: Multiple AP Indoor Localization via Mobile Phone Sensors(finished, in progress towards an expansion to a research project) YUMMY: HCI-based system improvement on a take-out platform(~full score in final presentation) A literature review of empirical engineering research in 2018. Full slides and report availble at here Nanjing University Exam Video Upload Website for Art Students(for Nanjing University Office of Academic Affairs) Bequank Financial Platform (for CitiCup 2018) GeekMark Crwodsourcing platform(Project for Course SEC-III) ServicesAcademic Services Computer Network, TA I am the only bachelor in the TA group, and the author of a new version of experiment guide. Cyber Attack and Defence Experiment, TA I am the organizer of NJU TrinityCTF in 2018 and I gave lectures about forensic, cryptography and misc issues for sophomore students taking the course. Nanjing University Peer Mentor (2018-2020) I am the first batch of peer mentors in the university. Scholarships and Honors Nanjing University Outsanding Student, 2018 Nanjing University Outsanding Student, 2017 Renmin Scholarship, 2018 Renmin Scholarship, 2017 Zhenggang Elite Scholarship, 2017 Personal Interests I am good at both piano and violin, and am interested in making own transcribes of music to fit actual needs of performances: Violin‚ÄďMaster of Second Violin (2017-2019), Nanjing University Symphony Orchestra (2016- ) I am a member of Nanjing University Symphony Orchestra, and I had a great time in there with many brilliant people. Piano‚ÄďVice Chairperson(2017-2018), Nanjing University Lyreland Piano Club I have performed in many concerts and met with many great people. Currently I am learning and trying to make symphonic arrangements for famous ACG-related music. Please check my website https://space.bilibili.com/34264046 for future updates. Competitions Taken(Chronological) NOV 2018, CitiCup final, Dalian OCT 2018, Huwang Cup Industrial Internet Security Contest, Beijing MAY 2018, BCTF Final, Beijing MAR 2018, NJUSTCTF 1st, Nanjing U. of Sci. and Tech. JAN 2018, Linghang Internet Knowledge Contest, Nanjing DEC 2017, TrinityCTF 1st, NJU JAN 2017, EL Software Programming Contest, NJU Performances(Chronological) Concert Date Role Nanjing University Orchestra Anniversary Concert 2019 summer Vice Chief Violin Nanjing University Orchestra New Year Concert 2018 winter Vice Chief Violin Lyreland Pop music Piano Concert 2018 fall Piano Soloist Nanjing University Orchestra Anniversary Concert 2018 summer Chief Violin Lyreland Pop music Piano Concert 2018 spring Piano Soloist Nanjing University Orchestra New Year Concert 2017 winter Chief Violin Nanjing University Ten Top Persons Award Ceremony 2017 Oct. Piano Soloist Lyreland Pop music Piano Concert 2017 fall Piano Soloist Nanjing University Orchestra Chambers Concert 2017 Summer Piano Nanjing University Orchestra Anniversary Concert 2017 summer Chief Violin Lyreland Pop music Piano Concert 2017 spring Piano Soloist Nanjing University Orchestra New Year Concert 2016 winter Violin Nanjing University Thanksgiving Ceremony 2016 Piano Soloist Lyreland Pop music Piano Concert 2016 fall Piano Soloist

pages.title: People
pages.text: My advisorsFengyuan Xu, Full Professor at COSEC NJU, is my supervisor in my information security research projects.Hongyu Kuang, Assistant Professor at SWI NJU, is my supervisor in information rerieval research projects. My friendsHao Wu is a phd students who taught me valuable preliminary knowledges about how to do research and become ready to become a good phd student. Hua Meng is my girlfriend, who gave me great courage and support, helping me not only rediscover myself and insist on things I like, but also ge away from the mental problems that had bothered me for over six years. Xiangzhe Xu is my roomate, friend, and also teacher during my bachelor life. He is a self-motivated, perspective, active and capable student. He is also finding a phd position, and is currently on visit to CS department at Yale university as research assistant. wanz is my classmate who is currently in Microsoft at Suzhou. calabash_boy is my good friend, a four gold-medal winner in ACM. He is in internship in Google. Angel_Kitty is a friend of mine in CTF competitions

posts.title: RELEASE! MAIL-Multiple AP Indoor Localization project
posts.text: RELEASE! MAIL-Multiple AP Indoor Localization projectfrostwing98(Yuqing Yang)COSEC, Nanjing University I. IntroductionAs the widespread deployment of various access points(hereinafter AP), more and more people begin to enjoy the convenient life of surfing wherever they are. The APs are set in homes, offices, coffee shops, shopping malls, and a large number of other public or private scenes. However, this led us to a question: are these APs safe ? The answer is for sure: APs are not always official, and some of them are rather BAD. Due to the fact that mobile phones usually re-connect to the APs which thay used to connect merely by the AP name and the signal strength, attackers usually utilize such feature to set up a fake AP(herein after rogue AP), with the same name, same MAC etc. Such rogue APs have stronger signal levels and are able to ‚Äėkick out‚Äô the official APs, to lure the victim‚Äôs phones to initialize the reconnection process according to the name and strength, i.e., to connect to the attacker‚Äôs rogue AP. Then the attacker‚Äôs rogue AP(could be a mobile hotspot, PC or even a real router) act as a middle man between the victim and the real AP. By launching a man-in-the-middle attack(henceforth mitm attack), capturing the packets sent from victims or the official AP, the attackers can easily eavesdrop the credential information from the victims, such as passwords. Prior to implementing the project, I made a fake Nanjing University WLAN authentication System. A fake website example And the original one: The original website II. Related worksTo tackle such a problem, researchers have developed various approaches. Radiometric signatures(2008)Vladmir Brik et. al., [1]utilized radiometric signatures to identify the subtle differences between two different APs but with same names. Usually a router‚Äôs radiometric signatures, exempli gratia, strengths, although vary but tiny, stay stable during long term runs. However such fluctuation in radiometric signature between devices, even same types of same devices, are rather different. Timestamp based(2008,mobicom)Suman Jana et al., [2]proposed a new timestamp-based way. Rather than TCP/IP timestamp, their approach measures TSF(Time Synchronization Function) timestamp, which is generated by router itself, with a high granularity upto 1ms, needless of considering delays and hard to manipulate, to measure the arrival of packet. They developed a Linear Programming Method(LPM) to avoid delay, and a Least Square Fitting(LSF) algorithm to estimate them. The ‚Äėjump‚Äô of arrival time indicates that there is a hidden shadow router that may be eavesdropping. RTT-based(2009,infocom)Hao Han et al., [3] take RTT time of DNS lookup non-recursive query into consideration. Their research are based on these sound assumptions: Most local networks has a local DNS server. A station can always send a dns-lookup request. DNS request cannot be blocked by networkproviders. DNS response varies for different queries. Since the user-generated DNS queries are unpredictable, it is impossible for rogue AP to keep up a huge database of DNS addresses of massive entries, even if the attacker has taken dns query into consideration when setting up rogue AP. Henceforth the Ap is forced to forward the query to another dns server, and there is a time delay. III. Baseline analogy: phone as a radarThe researches on rogue AP detection has been through for over ten years. However the story is not over. Let‚Äôs take this step forward. If we have already suspecting a presence of rogue AP in the adjacency, what should we do? The answer is simple: let‚Äôs locate it. In 2011, Zengbin Zhang et al.[4] proposed an idea of merely using off-the-shelf smartphone, and without special equipments, to locate on which direction is the connected AP placed. The principle is simple. When the user stands in front of the wireless antenna of the smartphone, the emissions of phone may be blocked. Hence the signal strength received from the AP decreases.In such case, the user merely needs to turn around 360 degree like a radar, and signal from all blocking stances is recorded. The degree at which the signal decreses most dramatically becomes the direction of AP. Antenna working process IV. Our method: phone as a Geiger counterThe inspiration of my method, however, comes from two interesting stories. The Chernobyl DisasterThe explosion of Chernobyl is a disaster, and even until today, those who enters Chernobyl should be equipped with a Geiger counter. When holding the geiger counter, it shows the current radiation, when the radiation rises, it indicates that we are walking towards a center of concerntrated radiating materials.What about we apply such scheme to localizing APs? Checking-in in enterprisesSome companies now adopt checking in via phone. When checking-in, the environmental signature, e.g., AP names and strength around, GPS localization informations, will be take into account to verify that you are IN the company, rather than FAKING TO BE in the company. This process is also related to indoor-localizing people using multiple routers. In a paper in 2016 from MSR-India, a trangular localization algorithm can be adopted using multiple APs to localize user. Okay, so what about walking around the room, sampling and analyzing the signals, then PINPOINT the AP?This is the method. The MAIL project. V. Project DesignThe original project is a SAIL project, single Ap indoor localization. I want to test whether it is possible detecting a given AP. Sensing Wifi Conditional wifi sensor The original design takes wifi information once upon receiving it, creating a lot of system overeads. This is bad because the app easily stops working. Hence in the final version, the sensing stage works with a interval of 4 seconds, or when the user manually force the process. Sensing movement Manual step Originally I used accelerator to sense steps, and Magnetic field sensor to sense orient. However the accelerator sucks(:P) hence in the final version, the step is performed manually. User clicks ‚Äėrecord‚Äô when the orientation is adjusted to a correct condition, then the virtual step appears on screen. Sensor Passivation Originally the sensor was too accurate and sensitive. A factor of 2-5 degree is introduced. Without a change in 2-5 degrees, the direction will not be changed. Data analysis Smoothing The idea comes from the convolution neural network. A matrix is adopted to calculate means of each point and their adjacent point. The purpose is to minimize the influence of noises, or sudden fluctuations of signal strength. Regression According to the scene in which the user is required to go straight or turn without going around, the data should be subject to a cubic polynomial pattern. Hence a cubic polynomial regression is performed. Pinpointing To pinpoint, the SAIL first calculates the point with maximal strength, then a floating window of +/- 2.5 strength is applied. Points within this window are in the predicted range. A circle enclosing all these points is then drawn. The AP is pinpointed. VI. Something moreDuring the experiment, I found that the system is even able to process multiple APs. By adding them all into a Map in each step, it is capable of pinpoint multiple APs. Therefore the project is upgraded into MAIL. Moreover, the system has the potential of not merely pinpointing APs, but also distinguish device type, and even applicable of pinpointing non-APs, i.e. clients. The future PC version of implementing above features is in progress. VII. EvaluationsIn the experiments both in dormitory and classroom, the granularity of localization can be within 1 meter, which is quite good. Experimantal result (step length is around 50 cm, time between each step is less than 0.5s) Compared with I am the antenna, our method does not require standing and rotating, which attracts the sight of strangers, causing alert to attackers, and we are capable of pinpointing rather than merely telling the direction. Our method is stealthy, even able to locate when putting the phone in pocket, and capable of locating multiple APs. VIII ResourcesThe code is available on Github: https://github.com/frostwing98/MAIL-IndoorAPLocalization IX AcknowledgementMany thanks to Associate Prof. Jingyu Hua, who gave me plenty guidances and suggestions in this project. References[1] [Vladimir Brik et al., 2008]Wireless Device Identification with Radiometric Signatures [2] [Suman Jana et al., 2008]On Fast and Accurate Detection of Unauthorized Wireless AccessPoints Using Clock Skews [3] [Hao Han et al., 2009]A Measurement Based Rogue AP Detection Scheme [4] [Zengbin Zhang et al., 2011]I am the antenna: accurate outdoor AP location using smartphones

II. śĘóś¶ā‚Äč Śú®2019ŚĻī7śúąšĽĹÔľĆśąĎŤé∑ŚĺóÁöĄÁ¨¨šłÄšĽĹťĚěś≠£ŚľŹŚćöŚ£ęÁĒüťĚĘŤĮēšł≠ÔľĆśąĎŤĘęťóģŚÄíšļÜŤŅôś†∑šłÄšł™ťóģťĘėÔľöhow do you think of your university life?śąĎŚõěÁ≠ĒÔľĆśąĎťĚěŚłłśĽ°śĄŹÔľĆŚõ†šłļŚú®ŤŅôŚá†ŚĻīÔľĆśąĎŚá†šĻéŚįĚŤĮēšļÜšłÄŚąáśąĎśÉ≥ŚĀöÁöĄšłúŤ•ŅÔľĆŚĻ∂šłĒŚĀöÁöĄšłćťĒô„ÄāśąĎŚá†šĻéś≤°śúČšĽĽšĹēťĀóśÜĺÔľĆŚõ†ś≠§śąĎŚŹĮšĽ•ŤĹĽśĚĺÁöĄÁĽßÁĽ≠ŚźĎŚČć„Äā ‚Äč Śú®ŤŅáŚéĽÁöĄ3ŚĻīÔľĆśąĎÁĽŹŚéÜšļÜťęėŤÄÉÔľĆśąźšłļšļÜÁŹ≠ÔľąÁļßÔľČťēŅÔľĆŚú®Ś≠¶ÁĒüšľöŚíĆśĖįŚ™íšĹďŚļ¶ŤŅášļÜśēīśēīšłÄŚĻīÔľĆŚĀöŤŅáťíĘÁźīÁ§ĺÁöĄŚČĮÁ§ĺťēŅÔľĆšļ§ŚďćšĻźŚõĘÁöĄŚįŹśŹźÁźīŚ£įťÉ®ťēŅŚíĆť¶ĖŚł≠ÔľĆśČďŤŅáŚģČŚÖ®śĮĒŤĶõÔľĆŚĀöŤŅáŚä©śēôÔľĆšłäŤŅášłďšłöŤĮĺÔľĆšĽ£ŤŅáŚźąŚĒĪśĆáśĆ•ŤĮĺÔľĆśźěŤŅáÁßĎÁ†ĒÔľĆŚĀöŤŅáŤģ≤ŚļßÔľĆŚíĆśĚ•Ťá™ŚźĄÁßćťôĘÁ≥ĽÁöĄŚźĆŚ≠¶śąźšłļśúčŚŹčÔľĆśúÄťáćŤ¶ĀÁöĄÔľĆśĒ∂Ťé∑šļÜÁč¨ŚĪěšļéŤá™Ś∑ĪÁöĄÁąĪśÉÖ„Äā ‚Äč śąĎšĻüŚ∑ģšłÄÁāĻśĆāśéČšļÜŚĺģÁßĮŚąÜšļĆÔľĆś≤ČŤŅ∑ŤŅáśłłśąŹÔľĆśúČŤŅáśĽ°ŤĮĺÁöĄŚī©śļÉśó•Ś≠źÔľĆšĻüśúČšłÄŚ§©ŚĺÄŤŅĒšł§šł™ś†°ŚĆļšł§ś¨°ŚĚźšļÜ80kmÁöĄŚúįťďĀÁöĄśó•Ś≠ź„ÄāŤŅôšļõÁĽŹŚéÜśúÄÁĽąťÉĹśĹúÁßĽťĽėŚĆĖšł≠ŚÜ≥ŚģöšļÜśąĎŚįÜśąźšłļšĽÄšĻąś†∑ÁöĄšļļ„Äā ‚Äč ŤŅôÁĮáśĖáÁę†šłćšľöśēôšĹ†Ś¶āšĹēśąźšłļśĽ°ÁĽ©Ś§ßšĹ¨ÔľĆšĻüšłćšľöśėĮšłÄÁĮáśĖįÁĒüśĆáŚćó„ÄāŤŅôśėĮšłÄťÉ®śôģťÄöÁöĄÔľĆŚÖ≥šļéšłÄšĹćśôģťÄöÁöĄŚźĆŚ≠¶Ś¶āšĹēŚú®Á£®ťöĺšĻčšł≠śÖĘśÖĘŚŹėŚĺóśąźÁÜü„ÄĀŤá™šŅ°„ÄĀŚÖÖśĽ°śĄüśŅÄÁöĄśēÖšļč„Äā ‚Äč ŚÖ®śĖáŚú®ŚģĆśąźšĻčŚźéšľöŤŅõŚÖ•ŤčĪśĖáŚĆĖÁöĄťė∂śģĶ„ÄāŚú®ťā£šĻčŚźéÔľĆŚŹĆŤĮ≠ÁČąśú¨ŚįÜšľöŤĘęŚŹĎŚłÉ„Äā ‚Äč This article will be translated into English once after it is finished. Both language‚Äôs versions will be published afterwards.

posts.title: Printer driver on Linux
posts.text: Linux DriverToday, my dear father sent the Lenovo printer to my dorm. After several minutes of busy installing the device, I sat down, brewed a cup of tea, and said, ‚ÄėLet‚Äôs see what this little girl can do!‚Äô Sadly, for the sake of goddamned linux operating system, a warning bumped out, saying, ‚ÄėPrinter driver not found.‚Äô Okay, I started searching using system-embedded tool. All right, it asks me to choose a brand but no freaking Lenovo brand found. How come? I started searching on the Internet. Good, Lenovo printers are mostly manufactured by Brother, and then re-branded to Lenovo. The model of my Lenovo printer is LJ2650DN, so I went to search ‚Äė2650‚Äô on Brother‚Äôs official website, hoping to find a linux driver. Damn bingo, the corresponding model is 2650DN, the picture of which looks exactly the same! Downloaded, specified model name and device uri, and fuck yeah, I can print my papers in resolution of 2400dpi! Never got to go to the printing shop any more. ](http://101.132.38.91/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/1.png) ![ Victory of linux user again!

posts.title: Solving [Waiting for headers] when using apt-get update
posts.text: Problem: When using apt-get update, always stuck @ following conditions: [Waiting for headers] or [Waiting for headers] [Connecting to security.ubuntu.com (2001:67c:1360:8001 Solution 1:Such problem could happen when ipv6 is enabled while software source doesn‚Äôt support. We can force apt-get use ipv4 method to download by using command as followed: sudo apt-get -o Acquire::ForceIPv4=true update Solution 2:Such problem could happen when previous software sources have been updated partially, when being too eager to press Ctrl+C, can cause integrity problems. Such problems could be solved by using: sudo rm -r /var/lib/apt/lists/sudo rm -r /var/lib/apt/lists/partial/ Solution 3:By removing all useless or expired software sources, PPAs, and anything else.

posts.title: English Posts From Today On
posts.text: English Posts from Today OnI hadbeen considering using English, a more wide-accepted language, to write down and publish my new posts. Such ideas had been stronger and stronger after I returned back from University of Oxford, and University of Cambridge, where I met with lots of new friends. They said that they were, and are, really interested in me, and my major. It is indeed a pity, though, that it happened to be a personal website written in Chinese: but not anymore. Moreover, during the days in UK, I became worried about my writing abilities. Although I have been quite confident in communicating with people fluently, my writing ability remained relatively weak. Then I came into a cool idea, that I will reorganize this lovely website, and adjust them in the favor of more friends from English-spoken countries or not, without the problems and barriers of languages. This is also the initial reason why I set up this website. From today on, I am going to change this website into English, and from now on, most of the posts shall be written in English.

posts.title: [VPN Again] Solving pip conflict when installing  shadowsocks on VPS
posts.slug: [VPN Again] Solving pip conflict when installing  shadowsocks on VPS
posts.path: 2018/07/25/[VPN Again] Solving pip conflict when installing  shadowsocks on VPS/
posts.permalink: http://yoursite.com/2018/07/25/[VPN Again] Solving pip conflict when installing  shadowsocks on VPS/
posts.text: Something about configuration VPSÔľö server refers to the public ip address, e.g. 138.128.206.46 server port refers to the serverport of the client, in this post, the port is 8888ClientÔľö serverIP correspond to the server of the VPS; port correspond to the server port of the VPS Something about damn CentOS the yum of centos is based on python2.7, however pip is based on python 3.6 So you may need: sudo yum install centos-release-scl sudo yum install rh-python36 scl enable rh-python36 bash to install shadowsocks using pip3.

int p,q; int sum=25*25-1,value=0,temp,ans; int state=1; ifstream input; int map[25][25]; bool flag[25][25]={0}; input.open(‚Äúdata.txt‚ÄĚ,ios::in); for (int i=0;i<25;i++) for (int j=0;j<25;j++) input>>map[i][j]; input.close(); p=0;q=1; ans=value=map[0][0]; flag[0][0]=1; while (1) { if (x+p=25 || y+q=25 || flag[x+p][y+q]) state=(state+1)%4; switch(state) { case 0:p=-1; q=0; break; case 1:p=0; q=1; break; case 2:p=1; ans+=value/temp*map[x][y]; } value=map[x][y]; flag[x][y]=1; sum‚Äď; } cout<<ans; return 0;}‚Äč ŤŅźŤ°Ćśą™ŚõĺÔľö Crypto1 Classical Encby frostwing98 ťĘėŚĻ≤Ôľö Ld hsrakwusyaxr, y hoyjjlhyo hlaxes lj y kraewv hlaxes kxyk zyj tjei xljkwslhyoor gtk dwz xyj vyooed, vws kxe pwjk aysk,ldkw iljtje. posts.title: [Solution] 404 Article after customizing wordpress permalink
posts.permalink: http://yoursite.com/2018/03/08/[Solution] 404 Article after customizing wordpress permalink/
posts.text: Solving 404 article problemsAfter customized permalink of the articles, the title of post remains but displays 404 page after clicking onto the title. The reason is that apache2 does not seem to support rewrite function, so we have to turn it on manually. If the wordpress is configured by LAMP, we need to simply edit two items as follows. Step1 a2en_mod rewrite (rewrite) Step2 Edit /etc/apache2/apache2.conf, find and modify: Options FollowSymLinksAllowOverride All(Initially:None) <Directory /var/www/html># ‚Ä¶ other directives‚Ä¶AllowOverride All(Initially:None) then:/etc/init.d/apache2 restart.
